title: Kadai-Roasted Granola

ingredients.name: desiccated coconut or grated fresh coconut

subs.name: puffed rice (murmura) for a lighter, crispier texture
any neutral cooking oil
subs.notes: coconut oil adds a pleasant coconut aroma

subs.name: honey or sugar (note: honey makes it non-vegan)

steps.text: Heat a wide kadai on medium-low flame. Add the oats, almonds, cashews and coconut together — do not add any oil yet. Dry-toast, stirring continuously, for 5 minutes until the oats begin to smell nutty and turn faintly golden.
Add the sesame seeds and toast for another 2 minutes, still stirring, until the seeds begin to pop. Lower the flame to its lowest setting before the next step.

Pour the melted coconut oil over the oat mixture and stir well so every piece is coated. Sprinkle in the grated jaggery, cardamom and salt. Stir constantly for 3–4 minutes — the jaggery will melt, coat the oats and caramelise slightly. Take off the heat the moment the mixture smells like toffee.

Spread the hot granola onto a clean plate or tray in a thin layer and let it cool completely — it will harden and clump as it cools. Do not stir while cooling or it will not cluster. Once fully cool, break into pieces and store in an airtight jar for up to 2 weeks.
Serve over cold curd, coconut milk or plain milk with fresh mango or banana slices on top.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: jaggery → honey or sugar (note: honey makes it non-vegan) (use 2 tbsp honey or sugar); coconut-oil → any neutral cooking oil (coconut oil adds a pleasant coconut aroma); almond → peanuts for a budget version (peanuts add a denser crunch); coconut → puffed rice (murmura) for a lighter, crispier texture
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This recipe is designed for the Indian kitchen — a wide kadai on a gas flame replaces the oven, jaggery replaces maple syrup, and coconut oil is readily available. Almonds and cashews are cheaper per kilo at Indian grocery stores than anywhere else. The finished granola keeps well in a steel dabba.
